message:
feat(core): add native fnmatch engine for fnmatch.h port (bd-fnm-1)

First sub-bead of bd-fnm-epic (port fnmatch.h to native Rust). The
existing engine lives in frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs as ~280
lines of pointer-arithmetic Rust; this commit lifts the algorithm
into a pure-safe slice-based core module ready for delegation in
bd-fnm-2.

frankenlibc-core/src/string/fnmatch.rs:
- pub fn fnmatch_match(pattern: &[u8], text: &[u8], flags: FnmatchFlags)
- pub struct FnmatchFlags with bit-compatible POSIX/glibc constants:
  NONE / PATHNAME / NOESCAPE / PERIOD / LEADING_DIR / CASEFOLD
- BitOr + BitOrAssign for natural flag combination
- classify_bracket() preserves the glibc 3-way unterminated-bracket
  semantics (Terminated / LiteralFallback / Invalid)
- fnmatch_inner() is the recursive matcher operating on slice indices
- Handles ?, *, [...], escapes, ranges, negation, leading-period
  blocking, PATHNAME slash blocking, casefold

16 unit tests pass:
- literal_match, empty_pattern_matches_empty_text
- question_mark_matches_one_byte, star_matches_zero_or_more
- bracket_class_matches_one_of, bracket_negation, bracket_range
- pathname_blocks_slash_in_star
- period_blocks_leading_dot_match, period_pathname_combined
- noescape_makes_backslash_literal, casefold
- leading_dir_allows_path_prefix
- unterminated_bracket_literal_fallback,
  unterminated_bracket_with_dash_invalid,
  bracket_close_first_is_literal

frankenlibc-abi rebuilds cleanly against new core module.

Closes bd-fnm-1; bd-fnm-2 will wire fl::fnmatch.
